sub_mechanisms: M_1: construct a d-regular Ramanujan graph on N nodes (LPS-1988 or Marcus-Spielman-Srivastava-2014)
M_2: assign one LLM agent per node
M_3: implement message-passing protocol along graph edges
M_4: bound mixing time via Alon-Boppana spectral gap
stripped_llm_application: A multi-agent LLM communication topology defined by a Ramanujan-bounded d-regular expander graph; sparse connectivity with provable spectral mixing properties; messages broadcast in O(log N) hops.
novelty_claim_stripped: Existing multi-agent systems use fully-connected (O(N^2) cost) or chain (O(N) diameter) topologies. The candidate uses an explicit Ramanujan-optimal expander with provable mixing properties.
